verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted serine protease traditionally described as a soluble protein in normal prostate tissue (secreted into prostatic fluid). However, 2025 preclinical studies have confirmed that KLK2 displays on the cell surface specifically in prostate cancer cells, as demonstrated by FACS, IHC, multiplex immunofluorescence, and confocal microscopy with colocalization to the cell surface marker EpCAM. The surface pool is cancer-cell-restricted and not present in baseline normal prostate epithelium, qualifying it as cell-state-induced surface display. Multiple cell-surface-directed therapeutics (bispecific T-cell engagers, CAR-T, radioligand therapy) have demonstrated preclinical efficacy against the surface-displayed form, establishing that the protein body is indeed accessible from the extracellular face in this oncogenic context.
